description_long: This object is a large, flat solar energy installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels mounted on a broad, platform-like base. The panels are arranged in a staggered, grid-like pattern, with some panels tilted at an angle to optimize sunlight capture. The base structure appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and reinforced materials, with several raised sections and blocky supports distributed across its surface. The installation includes both dark, grid-textured solar panel surfaces and lighter, metallic or concrete-like areas, possibly for structural reinforcement or access pathways. There are also several black, boxy components that may house electrical or mechanical equipment. The overall design suggests a robust, urban solar infrastructure element, likely intended for rooftop or large-scale ground deployment.
description: A large, platform-based solar energy installation with multiple tilted solar panels and robust structural supports, featuring a mix of dark panel surfaces and lighter base materials.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the installation, with two main groups of tilted solar panels and several raised black components.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the staggered arrangement of the solar panels and the blocky supports along the sides.
description_view_2: Opposite side view revealing the length of the base, the distribution of panels, and the contrasting red edge detail.
description_view_3: Reverse end view focusing on the panel grid layout and the symmetry of the supporting structures.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 25-30 degrees, with the tilt direction aligned along the long axis of the base; orientation appears fixed.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted or platform-mounted with visible metal frame and blocky support structures, likely aluminum or steel.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple panels arranged in a staggered, grid-like cluster, approximately 2 main groups with 3-4 panels each.
